content.text: This use case considers the safety integrity of operations performed by a TriCore CPU on the non-volatile program and data memories (PFLASH and DFLASH or PRRAM and DRRAM

rows: ESM[SW]:NVMR:CONFIG_READBACK
The software shall detect faults in the RRAM configurations by reading back the configured registers and checking against the expected values
NA
NA

A mismatch might be due to transient or permanent register faults or due to a software failure during the writing of the registers. It is recommended to write the registers again and repeat the check; if the error persists, it is recommended to do a system reset.
ESM[SW]:NVMR:PNVM_INTEGRITY_CHECK

Within multi-point fault detection time interval, the application software shall compare the PRRAM checksum of all safety related code with an expected PRRAM checksum. If the calculated and expected PRRAM checksums do not match, the application software shall trigger an alarm
NA
NA

On detection of CRC mismatch or an error buffer overflow of the DEAB/BFAB and UBAB registers, the content of the PRRAM cannot be trusted anymore. Safety alarms allow to detect which PRRAM bank is affected. The application software shall trigger an alarm and decide on an appropriate reaction
ESM[SW]:NVMR:EEPROM_DRIVER_D

After read of safety related RRAM.EEPROM content and before using the data in safety related function, the application software shall validate the RRAM.EEPROM data consistency with an expected RRAM.EEPROM information redundancy (for example stored in RRAM.EEPROM). The expected information redundancy shall also cover the target address of the data storage. If the calculated and expected RRAM.EEPROM information redundancy do not match, the application software shall trigger an alarm
NA
NA

the RRAM operations at correct operating conditions lead again to errors then the RRAM region cannot be used anymore and the application software shall trigger an alarm and decide on an appropriate reaction
ESM[SW]:NVMR:UCB_READBACK

programming a new value in a UCB, the [application SW] shall detect a fault in the UCB or a failure of the UCB writing process by reading back the configured UCB and checking against the expected value. Upon detection of a mismatch, the [application SW] shall rewrite the UCB if possible and repeat the ESM. If the error persists or a rewrite is not possible then the content of this UCB cannot be trusted anymore and shall not be used during the next MCU start-up. For dual UCBs with ORIG and COPY versions, the ORIG is evaluated first; if the fault happened during the update of the UCB COPY and the content of the UCB ORIG is still readable as configured then a start-up after the next reset is still possible . If the fault happened during the update of the UCB ORIG then it must be ensured that the content of the UCB COPY is still readable with the configured content. If the content of the UCB COPY is still readable with the configured content then the update of the UCB ORIG can be repeated
NA
NA
is recommended to trigger an alarm and decide on further appropriate reaction
SM[HW]:NVMR:MISR

This safety mechanism monitors continuously the RRAM configurations stored in register banks (SFR read and write settings, wordline redundancy and bitgroup redundancy). The RRAM configurations are checked continuously preventing the faults from becoming latent The monitoring is realized using several instances of an 8 bit multiple input signature register (MISR) calculating cyclically a unique polynomial signature over the register bank entries
Available in read mode after application reset.

If an error is detected in the static configuration of PMUR, the alarm PMUR static configurations MISR error is raised. If an error is detected in the static configuration of DMUR, the alarm DMUR static configurations MISR error is raised.
SM[HW]:NVMR:PNVM_ERROR_MANAGEMENT

The PRRAM ECC error management safety mechanism also commonly called error address buffer will Monitor single located ECC byte errors (single erasure) in the read data of the PRRAM Monitor double located ECC byte errors (double erasure) in the read data of the PRRAM and send an alarm when technology dependent threshold is reached Monitor the unlocated ECC byte errors (bit-flip) in the read data of the PRRAM and send an alarm when technology dependent threshold is reached Monitor the uncorrectable byte errors in the read data of the PRRAM and send an alarm
This safety mechanism is active after application reset.
Upon detection of the address buffers being full, the respective alarms are generated, except for SEAB buffers, the do not have an overflow alarm
SM[HW]:NVMR:PNVM_ECC_ONLINE_MONITOR

The PRRAM stores data with Error Correcting Codes (ECC) in order to protect the read data and address against corruption. The PRRAM ECC has the ability to detect and correct located '6-bit group' errors (erasure error) and unlocated '6-bit group' errors (bit-flip error).
This safety mechanism is active after application reset.
If the alarm is due to a PRRAM ECC online monitor error, it is recommended to do a application reset
SM[HW]:NVMR:PNVM_WAIT_STATE_PROTECTION

The safety mechanism checks for unintentional changes in the wait state configuration of the PMUR to protect the PRRAM read timings. The wait state configuration is protected using an Error Correction Code (ECC) mechanism with single bit error and double bit error detection capabilities
This safety mechanism is active after application reset.
Upon detection of a fault in the wait state configuration observed by ECC, an alarm is generated by the counter part SM[HW]:CPU:CFI_WAIT_STATE_PROTECTION
SM[HW]:NVMR:REG_MONITOR

This mechanism also commonly called safety flip-flop (SFF) detects and corrects transient and permanent faults in configuration registers and internal safety relevant states of the RRAM by using Triple Modular Redundancy (TMR) Safety Flip-flops (SFF).
alarm to the SMU is generated
